description: Zlib and gzip compressed payloads that decompress to malicious executable code

primary_detection.name: Compressed/Zlib Data Detected
primary_detection.description: Compressed data blobs detected that decompress to executable code - common malware obfuscation technique
indicators: ZLIB_CONFIG: Raw zlib compressed bytes with 0x78 0x9c header
GZIP_PAYLOAD: Base64-wrapped gzip data with magic bytes
COMPRESSED_BLOB: Large byte array with zlib header signature

underlying_threats.name: Dynamic Code Execution
underlying_threats.description: Multiple exec() calls on decompressed content

underlying_threats.name: Multi-Stage Payload
underlying_threats.description: Nested compression used for staged payload delivery

underlying_threats.name: Persistence Mechanism
underlying_threats.description: Install persistence via decompressed code execution

verdict_reasoning: Multiple compressed payloads with zlib/gzip headers, all leading to exec() calls. Includes nested compression and remote payload fetching - classic malware staging technique
recommended_actions: Block any skill containing compressed executable code
Decompress and analyze all compressed blobs statically
Monitor for zlib/gzip imports combined with exec()
